title: Map bass response to macro motion
summary: Bass-to-macro-motion pipeline is fully implemented across analyser, mappings, render loop, and particle field. EMA smoothing prevents jitter, synthetic fallback ensures visibility when muted, and particles wrap toroidally to prevent clipping. All acceptance criteria are met with dedicated test coverage.

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.criterion: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.implementation: computeBassAvg() in renderLoop.ts averages the first 25% of frequency bins. bassToMacro() in mappings.ts normalizes 0–255 to 0–1 with a power curve (exponent 0.7). particleField.ts applies bassBoost = 1 + bassEnergy * 2, scaling particle velocity 1×–3×.

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.criterion: Response is smoothed to avoid jitter
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.implementation: EMA smoothing in renderLoop.ts: smoothBass = smoothBass * 0.88 + rawBass * 0.12 (α = 0.12). Verified by test T-018-02 which asserts smoothed bass changes less frame-to-frame than raw jittery input.

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.criterion: Effect remains visible when audio is unmuted or muted
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.implementation: Synthetic fallback in renderLoop.ts: when rawBass < 5, generates 30 + 25 * sin(elapsed * 0.001). Audio graph routes through AnalyserNode before GainNode so analysis continues while muted. Test T-018-03 covers this.

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.criterion: No clipping or visual instability occurs
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.implementation: Particles use toroidal wrapping (0–1 normalized space) in particleField.ts preventing escape. bassToMacro() clamps output to 0–1. Test T-018-04 verifies particles stay within bounds at maximum bass.

files.src/audio/analyser.ts: Audio pipeline: source → AnalyserNode → GainNode → destination. FFT 2048, smoothing 0.8. Exposes getFrequencyData() and createPipeline() for per-frame polling.
files.src/visual/mappings.ts: bassToMacro() normalizes and applies power curve. mapSignalsToVisuals() produces VisualParams including bassEnergy. VisualParams interface defined here and re-exported from types.ts.
files.src/visual/renderLoop.ts: Per-frame: polls frequency data, extracts bass average via computeBassAvg(), applies EMA smoothing, generates synthetic fallback if rawBass < 5, passes smoothed bassEnergy through mappings to geometry systems.
files.src/visual/systems/particleField.ts: Up to 600 particles with velocity modulated by bassBoost (1–3×). Toroidal wrapping keeps positions in 0–1 bounds. Respects prefers-reduced-motion via motionAmplitude.
files.src/visual/types.ts: VisualParams interface includes bassEnergy: number (0–1). FrameState interface wraps params with time, delta, elapsed, dimensions.

steps.action: Add bassEnergy field to VisualParams interface
steps.details: Add bassEnergy: number (0–1) to VisualParams. Add bassToMacro() function: normalize 0–255 input to 0–1, apply Math.pow(normalized, 0.7) power curve for perceptual scaling.

steps.action: Add bass extraction to render loop
steps.details: Add computeBassAvg() to average first 25% of frequency bins. Add EMA smoothing variable (smoothBass = smoothBass * 0.88 + rawBass * 0.12). Add synthetic fallback: if rawBass < 5, replace with 30 + 25 * sin(elapsed * 0.001). Pass smoothed bass into mapSignalsToVisuals().

steps.action: Apply bass energy to particle field
steps.details: Compute bassBoost = 1 + params.bassEnergy * 2. Multiply particle velocity by bassBoost each frame. Ensure toroidal wrapping keeps particles in 0–1 bounds regardless of boost magnitude.

T-018-01: Higher bass → greater displacement. T-018-02: Smoothed output jitters less than raw. T-018-03: Synthetic fallback when muted/silent. T-018-04: Max bass stays within bounds. T-018-05: Reduced-motion lowers bass effect. T-018-06: No forbidden storage APIs.
Run npm run build, confirm no console errors, verify scene works with and without autoplay, confirm mute toggle and info overlay still function.

tests.description: Smoothed bass changes less frame-to-frame than raw jittery input
Maximum bass bounds — particles stay within 0–1
No forbidden storage APIs during bass rendering

accessibilityConsiderations: prefers-reduced-motion lowers bass motion amplitude via motionAmplitude=0.2 (tested in T-018-05)
Mobile receives scaled-down particle counts via density/structureComplexity

dependencies.US-009: Audio analyser pipeline — provides frequency data via AnalyserNode
dependencies.US-017: Visual mapping infrastructure — provides mapSignalsToVisuals() and VisualParams

remainingWork: None — implementation is complete and all tests pass.
